fields.label: Region
fields.description: This Atlas instance's single region (e.g. blr1, nyc3). The one source of truth for region: the proxy-fleet join key Subdomain/Site/Port Mapping/proxy VMs share, the label that separates this bench's servers in a shared cloud account, and the region announced to Central at Register. Root Domain denormalizes it at insert.

fields.label: Provider Type
fields.description: The active compute vendor. atlas.get_provider() maps this to the registered Provider implementation. Switching it is refused while any non-Archived Server was provisioned through a different vendor.

fields.label: TLS Provider Type
fields.description: The active certificate issuer. The TLS registry maps this to the registered TlsProvider; Root Domain / TLS Certificate denormalize it at insert. Pairs with Lets Encrypt Settings for the ACME account.

fields.label: Fail Scripts
fields.description: Developer-mode fault injection (Fake provider only). Comma/newline-separated script names whose Tasks should be made to FAIL; `*` fails every script.

fields.label: Default User Image
fields.description: Base image a dashboard user's new machine is provisioned from. If unset, the single active image is used; if several are active, set this so placement is unambiguous.

fields.label: Default Bench Snapshot
fields.description: The golden bench Virtual Machine Snapshot a self-serve Site's backing VM is cloned from (plan 01). The snapshot carries the preinstalled bench, MariaDB + Redis; Site.before_insert resolves it and provisions via Virtual Machine Snapshot.clone_to_new_vm. Must be set before any Site is created.

fields.label: Overprovision Factor
fields.description: Fleet-wide vCPU oversubscription multiplier. A host's effective vCPU budget is its physical total times this factor; placement (and the capacity display) check against that budget. 1 = no oversubscription. Servers whose size has no known vCPU total are unaffected — they are always treated as having room.

fields.label: TCP Port Pool
fields.description: Inclusive LOW-HIGH range of proxy-side ports the TCP forwarder allocates to Port Mappings (spec/17-tcp-proxy.md). MUST match the proxy's pre-opened nginx `listen` range; growing it is a deliberate proxy snapshot roll, not a live edit. Per region: the same port number is independently allocatable in each region.

fields.label: SSH Key ID
fields.description: Vendor's handle for the uploaded SSH key, passed to the provider when creating a host. DigitalOcean accepts either the key's numeric id or its SHA-256 fingerprint here.

fields.label: SSH Public Key
fields.description: OpenSSH public key body. Crosses the provider interface for vendors that upload at provision time.

fields.label: SSH Private Key Path
fields.description: Absolute path on the Atlas host where the SSH private key lives. 0600 owned by the Frappe user.
